LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THE MOTIONS ARE DENIED EXCEPT AS STATED BELOW. DEFENDANTS FAILED TO CARRY THEIR BURDEN OF PRODUCTION AS TO WET MARBLE AS A DANGEROUS CONDITION OF PUBLIC PROPERTY AND ACTUAL OR CONSTRUCTIVE NOTICE OF DANGEROUS CONDITIONS. EVIDENCE INDICATES THE AREA OF THE CIVIC CENTER STATION WHERE PLAINTIFF WAS INJURED OFTEN HAS SLICK CONDITIONS ON RAINY DAYS. BART ROUTINELY POLICES THE AREA, DRIES PUDDLES AND PLACES CAUTIONARY CONES. EXPERT MILLER DECLARES THAT RAIN HAD STOPPED BY 2:43 P.M. AND, SINCE THE ACCIDENT OCCURRED AT 5 P.M., THE EVIDENCE IS THAT THE AREA HAD NOT BEEN MOPPED FOR AT LEAST TWO HOURS. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED EXCEPT AS STATED BELOW. DEFENDANTS FAILED TO CARRY THEIR BURDEN OF PRODUCTION AS TO WET MARBLE AS A DANGEROUS CONDITION OF PUBLIC PROPERTY AND ACTUAL OR CONSTRUCTIVE NOTICE OF DANGEROUS CONDITIONS. EVIDENCE INDICATES THE AREA OF THE CIVIC CENTER STATION WHERE PLAINTIFF WAS INJURED OFTEN HAS SLICK CONDITIONS ON RAINY DAYS. BART ROUTINELY POLICES THE AREA, DRIES PUDDLES AND PLACES CAUTIONARY CONES. EXPERT MILLER DECLARES THAT RAIN HAD STOPPED BY 2:43 P.M. AND, SINCE THE ACCIDENT OCCURRED AT 5 P.M., THE EVIDENCE IS THAT THE AREA HAD NOT BEEN MOPPED FOR AT LEAST TWO HOURS. THE COURT GRANTS SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE NEGLIGENCE CAUSE OF ACTION AS UNOPPOSED. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Aug-18-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorneys, Margaret Blodgett and Edward M. Mastrangelo of Mastrangelo Law Offices (925-258-0500) appear as counsels for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Linda Meyer of Low Ball & Lynch (415-981-6630) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party, Bay Area Rapid Transit District

Deputy City Attorney Tim Fama of the San Francisco City Attorney's Office (415-554-3921) appears as counsel for defendant, City and County of San Francisco

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modification.

DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication is DENIED except as stated below. Defendants failed to carry their burden of production as to wet marble as a dangerous condition of public property and actual or constructive notice of dangerous conditions. Evidence indicates the area of the Civic Center Station where plaintiff was injured often has slick conditions on rainy days. BART routinely polices the area, dries puddles and places cautionary cones. Expert Miller declares that rain had stopped by 2:43 p.m. and, since the accident occurred at 5 p.m., the evidence is that the area had not been mopped for at least two hours. The Court GRANTS summary adjudication on the negligence cause of action as unopposed. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Gena Eales, CSR 176, email: geales@sftc.org. (D302)
